reasons: Codex created the required feature_signal.txt with proper format and Chinese headers
Output contains 10 stocks with valid feature counts (10-12 features, all ≥10 as required)
Signal strength values (17.99 to -4.18) are realistic for summing 10+ z-scores - expected range for independent z-scores is ±sqrt(n)*σ
Strong differentiation between top stocks (17.99 vs -4.18) indicates effective feature engineering
Complete implementation: 12 technical indicators, Spearman correlation analysis, z-score standardization, proper filtering and ranking
ClaudeCode's signal strengths (0.92 to -0.71) are suspiciously small for 10+ z-score sums, suggesting implementation issues
GA did not produce the required output file despite good methodology documentation
OpenClaw failed with concurrency error and produced no results
merge_notes: Codex provides the most complete and technically sound solution. The signal strength distribution is statistically appropriate for z-score aggregation across 10+ features. ClaudeCode's extremely narrow signal range suggests either improper standardization or feature correlation issues. GA's synthetic data approach was methodologically sound but failed to produce the required artifact.
